The '''autonomous short-range transport (SRT) droid''', otherwise known as the '''forklift droid''', was a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] used to carry bulk cargo in large industrial areas such as droid factories.

==Characteristics==
[[File:SRT-NEGTD.png|thumb|left|200px|An SRT droid]]
The SRT droid comprised a dome-like head and a bulky body fitted with a set of heavy lifting arms and a pair of multiple-joint lifting arms. The [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] heavy arms were capped with restraining forks, and kept cargo secure. The upper, jointed arms were used for precise work, such as basic maintenance and clearing production lines of obstructions. The droid's repulsor drive was housed in its tail unit, and was capable of supporting up to ten times the droid's own weight.<ref name="NEGTD" />

SRT droids were utilized around the clock in brutal factory environments, and most burned out their original components in less than a year. Aside from cargo hauling, these droids operated as factory overseers, keeping lookout on production through their heat-shielded [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]]. The SRTs were in constant communication with factory central computers, though their artificial intelligence was low enough that they focused solely on the job in hand.<ref name="NEGTD" />

==History==
Created by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]], SRT droids saw use in the very factories in which they were constructed. Many variations on the design arose among dozens of competing companies, including a [[Publictechnic]] model that utilized a large, bin-like holder instead of the Baktoid model's flat pallet. The [[GoCorp/Legends|GoCorp]]/[[Utilitech/Legends|Utilitech]] collaboration included a fully enclosed, boxcar-like carrying area. Many such droids found work as garbage collectors across the galaxy.<ref name="NEGTD" />

[[File:SRTdroid-AOTC.png|thumb|right|250px|An SRT droid carries C-3PO through the droid foundry on Geonosis.]]
SRT droids were employed in the [[Geonosis droid factories/Legends|droid factories]] on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]. [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] encountered one prior to the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], though the SRT saw the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] as little more than an obstruction and deftly removed him from the vicinity—causing C-3PO to fall unceremoniously onto a conveyor belt below.<ref name="AOTC" /><ref name="FF107" />

Criminals were known to use SRT droids to their advantage, intercepting the transports' flight paths to smuggle contraband into their cargo. Inspired by such tactics, a squadron of [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]] hijacked a work crew of SRT droids during a stealth operation across enemy lines in the lead-up to the [[Battle of Byblos]].<ref name="NEGTD" />

Some SRT droids were utilized in the construction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]].<ref name="TFU" />
